title: issue/UX: no indication that download is being prepared when downloading file or folder from Open Terminal
author: Classic298
body: Larger / Longer downloads of large files or folders may take 30sec to prepare as a zip on the server side, but there's no indication that if you press Download that anything happens, leading users to press Download multiple times and triggering zip on Server side multiple times

title: feat: Adding X-OpenWebUI-Message-Id to OpenAI Additional Headers
author: nathan-hook
body:
### Check Existing Issues

- [x] I have searched all existing **open AND closed** issues and discussions and found none comparable to my request.

### Verify Feature Scope

- [x] I believe this feature request is appropriately scoped for the Issues section as described above.

### Problem Description

Please consider adding the X-OpenWebUI-Message-Id header to a connections additional headers.

As far as I can tell this behavior is only added to the inlet and outlet requests to pipeline servers, but with the deprecation of the pipelines framework we no longer have any access to this particular header value.

This header value is important to us since we are building an OpenAI gateway/mediator/proxy product and the user's request is the only time that we can record this specific value from Open WebUI.  We could use this value to query Open WebUI's message database for any feedback on a specific message's answer.

FWIW, here is a related Discussion about the X-OpenWebUI-Message-Id http header:
https://github.com/open-webui/open-webui/discussions/23384

### Proposed Solution

Adding the X-OpenWebUI-Message-Id http header to any OpenAI backend requests.

### Alternatives Considered

_No response_

### Additional Context

_No response_

title: bug: Background memory review not triggering when assistant response is tool-only or has empty content
author: EdwardSierra
body:
### Description
The background memory review feature () fails to trigger when the assistant's response contains empty content or utilizes tool calls without accompanying text.

### Technical Details
In , the function  implements a gate check (lines 425-427) that aborts the review process if the assistant's content is empty:

```python
assistant_content = assistant_message.get('content', '')
if not isinstance(assistant_content, str) or not assistant_content.strip():
    return
```

This prevents the background review task from being created in scenarios where the model is operating primarily through tool calls (e.g., updating memories via tools), even if the user has enabled the background review and the model supports memory capabilities.

Interestingly, the internal  function (which is skipped by this gate) already contains logic to extract content from messages regardless of type, suggesting this gate check is overly restrictive.

### Additional Configuration Issue
During testing, it was observed that environment variables intended to enable this feature (e.g., ) did not overwrite existing database values. The settings had to be manually updated in the database to take effect, which contradicts the expected behavior where environment variables seed/override configuration.

### Expected Behavior
1. The background memory review should be allowed to trigger even if the `content` field is empty, provided that other requirements (model capability, feature flags, and turn interval) are met.
2. Environment variables should correctly override or seed the configuration in the database as per the documentation.

### Steps to Reproduce
1. Enable  in the configuration (via environment variable).
2. Set  to 1.
3. Interact with a model that uses tool calls for memory and produces empty `content` in the response.
4. Observe that the background review task is not initiated, and note that the env var may not have applied to the DB.

title: FEAT: Context Compaction, define custom task model

title: FEAT: Context Compaction: Make % retained messages configurable
author: Classic298
body:
Since the token maximum for each model can be configured independently, a model with a 100k token limit versus 500k will mean the 500k model will receive much more tokens of the "50% retained messages"

adjustments to e.g. 30% or 20% would be nice if doable.

title: issue: content-filter refusal text becomes the RAG retrieval query when query generation returns no JSON
author: kwame-wbs
body:
### Check Existing Issues

- [x] I have searched for any existing and/or related issues.
- [x] I have searched for any existing and/or related discussions.
- [x] I have also searched in the CLOSED issues AND CLOSED discussions and found no related items. Closest existing items, neither of which covers this: #21888 (`<think>` output breaking the JSON extraction, mitigated by the `rfind` change) and PR #26951 (changes the *empty-list* fallback only; the raw-text fallback below is untouched by its diff).
- [x] I am using the latest version of Open WebUI (v0.10.2; I have also verified the affected code is unchanged on current `main` and `dev`).

### Installation Method

Docker

### Open WebUI Version

v0.10.2

### Ollama Version (if applicable)

N/A (OpenAI-compatible backend)

### Operating System

Linux (Kubernetes)

### Browser (if applicable)

N/A (backend behaviour, no frontend involvement)

### Expected Behavior

When the retrieval query-generation task call fails to produce usable queries (for example because the backend's content filter blocked it and replaced the output with a refusal message), retrieval should fall back to the last user message, as the existing `len(queries) == 0` fallback intends.

### Actual Behavior

The refusal text itself becomes the vector-search query. In `chat_completion_files_handler` (`backend/open_webui/utils/middleware.py`, [L1786-L1796 on v0.10.2](https://github.com/open-webui/open-webui/blob/v0.10.2/backend/open_webui/utils/middleware.py#L1786-L1796), identical on `main`):

```python
try:
    bracket_start = queries_response.rfind('{')
    bracket_end = queries_response.rfind('}') + 1

    if bracket_start == -1 or bracket_end == -1:
        raise Exception('No JSON object found in the response')

    queries_response = queries_response[bracket_start:bracket_end]
    queries_response = json.loads(queries_response)
except Exception as e:
    queries_response = {'queries': [queries_response]}   # raw model text becomes the query
```

A blocked response contains no `{`, so the `except` wraps the whole refusal string as the query list. Because the list is now non-empty, the proper last-user-message fallback at L1813 (`if len(queries) == 0: ...`) never fires. Retrieval then runs a similarity search for the literal string "I can't help with that request.", returning irrelevant or near-zero-relevance chunks, and the user's answer is silently degraded. The response also carries `finish_reason: "content_filter"`, which is ignored.

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Run Open WebUI v0.10.2 (Docker, default configuration) against any OpenAI-compatible endpoint whose provider can block a request with a content filter and return `finish_reason: "content_filter"` with a refusal message as the content (e.g. a gateway in front of AWS Bedrock Guardrails, or Azure OpenAI content filtering). Any deterministic stand-in works: a mock OpenAI endpoint that returns `{"choices":[{"finish_reason":"content_filter","message":{"content":"I can't help with that request."}}]}` for the query-generation call reproduces it exactly.
2. Create a knowledge collection with a few documents and attach it to a model. Leave retrieval query generation enabled (default; Admin Settings > Interface).
3. Start a new chat with that model and send any question, e.g. "Explain this week's key topics".
4. Open WebUI fires the internal query-generation task call (`### Task: Analyze the chat history to determine the necessity of generating search queries...`). Arrange for that call to be blocked by the provider's content filter (with the mock endpoint from step 1 this is deterministic).
5. Observe (via debug logs or tracing on `rag.query_collection` / the emitted `queries_generated` status event) that the retrieval query list is `["I can't help with that request."]` rather than falling back to the user's message.
6. Expected at step 5: `queries == []` so the fallback uses the last user message. Actual: the refusal string is used for the similarity search and irrelevant context is passed to the model.

### Logs & Screenshots

Observed live (OpenTelemetry/Langfuse trace, self-hosted deployment, backend = OpenAI-compatible gateway in front of AWS Bedrock with Guardrails):

- query-generation task call output: `"I can't help with that request."` with `finish_reason: "content_filter"`
- subsequent retrieval span input: `["I can't help with that request."]`
- retrieved chunk relevance score: `6.8e-05` (an unrelated document; with `rerank_threshold` 0 it was still injected as context)

### Confirmation

- [x] I have read and followed all instructions in `README.md`.
- [x] I am using the latest released version of Open WebUI.
- [x] Browser console logs are not applicable (server-side retrieval path; server-side evidence included above).
- [x] Container logs relevant to the path are included above (trace excerpts); the failure is silent in default logging, which is part of the problem.
- [x] I have provided every relevant configuration: default Docker setup, a knowledge collection attached to a model, retrieval query generation enabled (default), OpenAI-compatible backend able to return `finish_reason: "content_filter"`.
- [x] I have listed every relevant configuration, custom setting and environment variable that influences the setup (see Steps to Reproduce; no other non-default settings are involved).
- [x] I have documented step-by-step reproduction instructions that are precise, sequential and leave nothing to interpretation.

### Additional Information

Suggested fix, either or both of:

1. In the `except`, fall back to `{'queries': []}` instead of `{'queries': [queries_response]}` so the existing last-user-message fallback applies. Raw model text that failed JSON extraction is rarely a useful similarity query, and refusal text never is.
2. Check `finish_reason == 'content_filter'` on the query-generation response and treat it as "no queries" explicitly.

A related aggravator (happy to raise separately if useful): the task templates render chat history via `{{MESSAGES:END:6}}`, and `get_messages_content` includes **system-role** messages verbatim, so the model's system prompt is pasted inside the imperative `### Task:` wrapper. For deployments with strict prompt-injection filters this makes the query-generation call itself much more likely to be blocked, which is how we hit the fallback bug in the first place.

I am happy to submit a PR for the fallback change if the approach is agreeable.

title: FEAT: make per AI-call timeout configurable
author: Classic298
body:
AIOHTTP CLIENT TIMEOUT defines how long one entire assistant message can run for.

Since tool calls and reasoning - combined - can take a long time you need to put something like 1800s (30mins) as a timeout.

what is not configurable though is a timeout that STOPS the turn if the assistant doesn't reply anything.
In essence, what is needed, is a "stop if idle for X seconds" or "stop if this time to first token is not met"

a timer that ends the connection and stops the streaming if the AI is idle for X amount of seconds - truly idle, so not streaming anything.

This could be set to a default of 30-60 seconds and be configured in prod environments to 20seconds so users will get an error instead of having to wait for AIOHTTP_CLIENT_TIMEOUT seconds (30mins) for the response to die.

Why is this needed?
Because sometimes upstream providers open the connection but then nothing gets streamed.
This happens rarely but it does happen.
Other apps have an assistant idle timeout and i think Open WebUI could also use one.

title: ISSUE: Context Compaction Breaks Cache by not actually retaining the 40% retained recent messages
author: Classic298
body:
the 40% recent messages are supposed to be RETAINED and SENT with the summary to the model.

So basically

summary+recent messages+user prompt

which it does

BUT, only for the FIRST turn after a compaction.

On the NEXT turn after the compaction, the recent messages part is GONE, and it's just:

summary+user prompt
assistant message
second prompt after summary: user prompt

notice the first message triggering the summary no longer contains the recent messages block.

This

1) breaks caching
2) breaks continuity. On the second message after the summary, the model will no longer know how the conversation behaved in the last couple of messages, because the recent messages block is gone and the summary doesn't convey it as the model is instructed with summarizing only the block of messages that is to be summarized.

title: feat: Support S3 Server-Side Encryption (x-amz-server-side-encryption)
author: Vayks123
body:
### Check Existing Issues

- [x] I have searched all existing **open AND closed** issues and discussions and found none comparable to my request.

### Verify Feature Scope

- [x] I believe this feature request is appropriately scoped for the Issues section as described above.

### Problem Description

We are using Open WebUI iwith Amazon S3 as the storage provider. To comply with our security and compliance policies, all objects stored in S3 must be encrypted using server-side encryption with S3-managed keys (SSE-S3).

Currently, Open WebUI does not seem to include the x-amz-server-side-encryption: AES256 header when uploading files to S3. This causes "Access Denied" or "403 Forbidden" errors when the S3 bucket policy enforces encryption.

### Proposed Solution

Please allow adding custom headers or explicitly enable x-amz-server-side-encryption: AES256 for S3 uploads in the STORAGE_PROVIDER=s3 configuration.

### Alternatives Considered

_No response_

### Additional Context

_No response_

title: ISSUE: Context compaction boundary is turn-blind — it compacts the middle of a single assistant turn instead of whole prior turns
author: Classic298
body:
# Summary:
_find_compaction_boundary in utils/context_compaction.py splits the flattened message list by count (keep last ~40%, compact first ~60%), with no concept of a logical conversation turn. When one assistant turn expands into many tool-call / tool-result messages, the boundary falls inside that single turn, so its early tool calls/results get summarized away while later ones are kept — corrupting the structure of a turn rather than compacting completed prior turns.

# Where

open_webui/utils/context_compaction.py, _find_compaction_boundary (and its caller compact_messages_for_request).

```
keep_count = max(2, len(messages) * 2 // 5)   # keep last ~40%
split = max(1, len(messages) - keep_count)     # compact first ~60%
# while-loop only nudges past an orphaned tool result; no turn awareness
```

# Steps to reproduce:

Enable context compaction with a token threshold.
Run a single agentic turn that makes several tool calls (so the flattened history is >3 messages and trips the threshold).
On the next request, compaction triggers and the boundary lands within that one turn.
Expected: Compaction operates on whole completed turns — it should never split a single user→assistant(+tool calls)→ turn. Keep the most recent turn(s) intact; only summarize fully-completed earlier turns.

**Actual:** The split is purely count-based on flattened messages; a single heavy turn gets its interior tool calls/results shoved into the compacted slice.

**Impact**: Loss of tool-call/result coherence for the current work; combined with the unbounded summary payload, it feeds partial-turn tool output into the task model.

**Suggested fix:** Compute the boundary on turn units (group by user message → its assistant/tool messages), and choose a split that only ever falls on a turn boundary, keeping ≥1 most-recent complete turn.

title: ISSUE: `usage` token fields are summed across tool-loop calls (regression in 0.10.2), inflating `input_tokens` and triggering premature context compaction
author: Classic298
body:
## Summary

Since **0.10.2**, when an assistant turn makes multiple model calls (native tool/function calling), the per-call `usage` objects are **added together** into the stored message usage. Token fields like `input_tokens` are summed across calls, even though each call re-sends the (growing) conversation history — so the shared history is counted once per call.

The stored `input_tokens` therefore no longer represents the conversation's context size; it represents the sum of every call's prompt, which for an N-call turn roughly multi-counts the history N times. This is a display problem for the usage tooltip, but more importantly it is a **correctness problem for `context_compaction`**, which reads that value as the current context length and compacts far too early.

`0.9.6` did not have this behavior — it used last-write-wins.

## Regression

- Introduced in commit `caadfde` ("refac", 2026-06-29), first released in **0.10.2**.
- Present in both `main` (`0.10.2`) and `dev`.
- `0.9.6` middleware set assistant usage by **overwrite**: `usage = normalize_usage(raw_usage)` — the final call only.

## Root cause

`backend/open_webui/utils/response.py` — `merge_usage()` sums every key in `USAGE_TOKEN_KEYS`:

```python
USAGE_TOKEN_KEYS = {
    'input_tokens', 'output_tokens', 'total_tokens',
    'prompt_tokens', 'completion_tokens',
}

# merge_usage(), response.py:104+
for key in USAGE_TOKEN_KEYS | USAGE_COST_KEYS:
    if key in current_usage or key in incoming_usage:
        ...
        result[key] = current_value + incoming_value   # <-- additive for input_tokens too
```

The tool loop accumulates into a single usage via `merge_usage`:
- `backend/open_webui/utils/middleware.py` `usage = merge_usage(usage, raw_usage)` (main:4154, dev:4167), and the two `assistant_message['usage'] = merge_usage(...)` sites.

Across a tool loop the prompt grows monotonically (call 2 includes call 1's tool call + result, etc.), so:
- call 1 `input_tokens` ≈ 40k
- call 2 `input_tokens` ≈ 80k
- call 3 `input_tokens` ≈ 120k

Summing gives ~240k stored `input_tokens` for a turn whose real end-of-turn context is ~120k (and whose *conversation* is only ~40k of durable content). Summing `input_tokens` has no valid interpretation, because the history in each call is the same tokens re-sent, not new tokens.

## Impact: premature context compaction

`backend/open_webui/utils/context_compaction.py` `_exceeds_token_threshold()` (dev:229) uses the stored usage as the current context size:

```python
usage = messages[idx].get('usage') or (messages[idx].get('info') or {}).get('usage')
if isinstance(usage, dict) and usage.get('input_tokens'):
    total = int(usage.get('input_tokens') or 0) + int(usage.get('output_tokens') or 0)
    return total + _estimate_messages_tokens(messages[idx + 1:]) > threshold
```

With the summed value, a chat whose true context is well under the threshold (default `chat.context_compaction.token_threshold` = 80000) is treated as far larger and gets compacted early. A single turn with a few tool calls at moderate context is enough to trip it. Compaction then summarizes/drops history that never needed to be dropped, degrading the conversation.

It also inflates the per-message usage tooltip (a 3-call turn shows ~3× the tokens actually resident in context), and any downstream consumer that reads `message.usage.input_tokens` as a length.

## Steps to reproduce

1. Enable context compaction (`chat.context_compaction.enable = true`), default threshold.
2. Use a model with native function calling and at least one tool.
3. In one chat, send a message whose answer requires **2–3 tool calls**, with a moderately long context (say ~30–40k tokens).
4. Inspect the assistant message's stored `usage` (tooltip or `chat_message.usage`): `input_tokens` is roughly the **sum** of each call's prompt, not the context size.
5. Continue the chat: compaction fires far below the real threshold because `_exceeds_token_threshold` reads the inflated value.

## Expected vs actual

- **Expected:** stored `input_tokens` for an assistant turn reflects the context size going into the turn (the final call's prompt), so compaction fires at the real threshold. (This is what 0.9.6 did.)
- **Actual:** `input_tokens` is the sum across all tool-loop calls; compaction fires early.

title: bug: Python CI (Ruff Format) failing on dev since 2026-07-09 — two files no longer match ruff format
author: kobihikri
body:
Hi — thanks for Open WebUI, and apologies for the missing title prefix; fixed.

**Before anything else: your bot pointed me at #23902, #385 and #21783, and it was right to.** I've now read all three, and I want to be straight about what's actually new here and what isn't — because most of what I originally wrote had already been raised, and in one case already decided.

## What is new: the format gate is currently red on `dev`

This is a **regression from the last few days**, and it's the only part of this issue I think warrants your attention.

`Python CI / Ruff Format` (3.11 and 3.12) is failing on current `dev` HEAD `09d4cccb`, and has failed on the **last 7 consecutive runs** of the workflow. The last green run I can find is `d3ea8eb7` (2026-07-09).

Two files no longer match `ruff format` under your own settings (`line-length = 120`, `quote-style = "single"`):

```
$ ruff format --check . --exclude .venv --exclude venv
Would reformat: backend/open_webui/retrieval/vector/dbs/milvus_multitenancy.py
Would reformat: backend/open_webui/routers/files.py
2 files would be reformatted, 236 files already formatted
$ echo $?
1
```

In both cases it's the same small thing: a call that fits inside your 120-column limit had been split across lines. Nothing behavioural.

**#27030** runs `ruff format` on exactly those two files and turns the job green again — 2 files, +2/−6, no logic touched, and the diff is literally the formatter's own output. That PR is the entire point of this issue; if you merge it, this can be closed.

To be clear about scope: this is **not** the lint-rules question from #23902. It's the *formatter* check — the one gate you do have switched on — being red.

## What is NOT new, and which I am explicitly not re-litigating

- **#23902** (@Samreay, April) already reported that the backend code doesn't respect the ruff lint rules in `pyproject.toml`. **You closed it `not_planned`: *"Not a priority, we may eventually get to it."*** That's a completely legitimate call and it's your project — I'm not reopening it, and I've deliberately kept it out of #27030. I'll only add one measurement in case it's ever useful when you do get to it: the `select` list in `pyproject.toml` isn't merely unenforced, it's *overridden* — `--select=F` on the CI command line replaces it rather than intersecting with it, so the declared policy reports 2,533 findings while the gate reports 0. Samreay's point that newcomers "get hit with thousands of errors in pre-commit" is exactly what I ran into. No action requested.
- **#385** (@ThatOneCalculator, 2024) already covers the `*.disabled` workflow files. Closed. Nothing to add.
- **#21783** already covers backend test coverage. Closed. Nothing to add.

I should have searched closed issues before writing all that up, and I didn't. Sorry for the noise — the bot did a better job than I did.

## The one small thing I'd still gently flag

`.github/workflows/frontend.yaml` opens with the comment *"Frontend CI — Lint, format check, build, and unit tests"*, but there's no lint step in the file. Given #385 and #23902, I assume that's known and intentional; a stray comment isn't worth an issue of its own, so I mention it only in passing and won't push it.

Thanks for reading, and thanks for the project.

---

*Checked against `dev` @ `09d4cccb794e917ae769645d199111c8de5d5097`, ruff 0.15.21 (what `pip install "ruff>=0.15.5"` resolves to today), in a clean container.*

title: issue: Filter outlet() changes to structured assistant.output are not persisted after reload in v0.10.2
author: etiennegnome
body: ### Check Existing Issues - [x] I have searched for any existing and/or related issues. - [x] I have searched for any existing and/or related discussions. - [x] I have also searched in the CLOSED issues AND CLOSED discussions and found no related items (your issue might already be addressed on the development branch!). - [x] I am using the latest version of Open WebUI. ### Installation Method Other ### Open WebUI Version v0.10.2 ### Ollama Version (if applicable) v0.31.2 ### Operating System Linux Mint 21.3 ### Browser (if applicable) Firefox 152.0.5 ### Confirmation - [x] I have read and followed all instructions in `README.md`. - [x] I am using the latest version of **both** Open WebUI and Ollama. - [x] I have included the browser console logs. - [x] I have included the Docker container logs. - [x] I have **provided every relevant configuration, setting, and environment variable used in my setup.** - [x] I have clearly **listed every relevant configuration, custom setting, environment variable, and command-line option that influences my setup** (such as Docker Compose overrides, .env values, browser settings, authentication configurations, etc). - [x] I have documented **step-by-step reproduction instructions that are precise, sequential, and leave nothing to interpretation**. My steps: - Start with the initial platform/version/OS and dependencies used, - Specify exact install/launch/configure commands, - List URLs visited, user input (incl. example values/emails/passwords if needed), - Describe all options and toggles enabled or changed, - Include any files or environmental changes, - Identify the expected and actual result at each stage, - Ensure any reasonably skilled user can follow and hit the same issue. ### Expected Behavior When a Filter Function modifies the final assistant message in `outlet()` and returns the modified `body`, the final assistant response should remain identical after the chat is reloaded. For structured assistant messages, Open WebUI should either: 1. persist both `message.content` and `message.output[type="message"].content[type="output_text"].text`; or 2. normalize them into one canonical representation before saving. A footer appended by `outlet()` should therefore remain visible after page reload or after leaving and reopening the chat. ### Actual Behavior In Open WebUI v0.10.2: - changes made by `outlet()` to `assistant.output[type="message"].content[type="output_text"].text` are visible immediately in the UI; - those `output` changes are not persisted; - changes made to `assistant.content` are persisted in both the `chat_message` row and the embedded chat JSON; - after reload, the UI renders the original unchanged text from `assistant.output`, so the persisted footer in `assistant.content` is not shown. This happens with streaming enabled and disabled. ### Steps to Reproduce ### Minimal Reproducer I used a dedicated Filter Function named: ```text Outlet Persistence Probe v0.1.0 ``` It appends the fixed marker: ```text [OPENWEBUI_OUTLET_PERSISTENCE_PROBE:v0.1.0] ``` The Filter supports three write modes: ```text content_only output_only content_and_output ``` The relevant logic is equivalent to: ```python # Flat compatibility field assistant_message["content"] = visible_text_with_marker # Structured response field for item in assistant_message.get("output", []): if item.get("type") != "message": continue for part in item.get("content", []): if part.get("type") == "output_text": part["text"] = visible_text_with_marker ``` The Filter then returns the modified `body`. The full minimal reproducer can be attached to this issue. ### Test Configuration - Open WebUI: v0.10.2 - Backend: Ollama - Model: `gemma4:12b` - One new empty chat per test - Only the persistence probe Filter enabled - Other custom Filters disabled - Prompt: ```text Reply with exactly: PERSISTENCE-BASELINE ``` ### Steps to Reproduce 1. Install and enable the minimal Filter. 2. Set `write_mode=content_only`. 3. Open a new chat with `gemma4:12b`. 4. Send: ```text Reply with exactly: PERSISTENCE-BASELINE ``` 5. Observe the immediate UI. 6. Reload the page. 7. Repeat in new chats with: - `write_mode=output_only`; - `write_mode=content_and_output`, streaming enabled; - `write_mode=content_and_output`, streaming disabled. 8. Inspect `webui.db` in read-only mode for the assistant message. ### Results Write mode | Streaming | Marker visible immediately | Marker visible after reload | Persisted in content | Persisted in visible output_text -- | -- | -- | -- | -- | -- content_only | On | No | No | Yes | No output_only | On | Yes | No | No | No content_and_output | On | Yes | No | Yes | No content_and_output | Off | Yes | No | Yes | No The Action Function buttons remained visible in all v0.10.2 tests. ### Outlet Logs For `content_and_output` with streaming disabled, immediately before returning the body: ```text write_mode=content_and_output content_modified=True output_modified=True output_target_path='output[1].content[0].text' content_after_marker=True output_after_marker=True RESULT=body_returned ``` So the Filter successfully modified both representations before returning. ### Read-Only Database Inspection For the same assistant message after reload: ```text [chat_message row] content_len : 69 marker_in_content : True output_types : ['reasoning', 'message'] visible_output_len : 20 marker_in_visible_output: False [embedded chat JSON message] content_len : 69 marker_in_content : True output_types : ['reasoning', 'message'] visible_output_len : 20 marker_in_visible_output: False ``` For the `output_only` test after reload: ```text marker_in_content : False marker_in_visible_output: False ``` This proves that the `output` modification returned by `outlet()` is discarded or overwritten before final persistence. ### Analysis The observed persistence behavior is: ```text outlet-modified content -> persisted outlet-modified structured output -> not persisted ``` But after reload, the UI appears to render the unchanged structured `output` rather than the persisted `content`. As a result, a Filter cannot currently add a persistent footer, compliance label, watermark, redaction, or other final transformation to structured assistant messages in v0.10.2. This appears related to #26436 and #26952, but the reproduction path is different: - this issue uses a Filter Function `outlet()`; - no message-update API endpoint is called; - the problem occurs with an Ollama model; - it is reproduced with both streaming on and off; - the Filter modifies both `content` and structured `output` before returning the body. ### Requested Fix Please ensure that post-processed assistant messages returned by Filter `outlet()` are persisted consistently. Possible expected implementations: 1. persist the modified `output` returned by `outlet()` together with `content`; or 2. rebuild/normalize `output[type="message"].content[type="output_text"].text` from the final post-processed `content`; or 3. define and document one canonical field for Filter post-processing and ensure both persistence and reload rendering use it. [Outlet_Persistence_Probe_v0.1.0.py](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/29939609/Outlet_Persistence_Probe_v0.1.0.py) ### Logs & Screenshots ### Additional Information ### Additional Information This behavior did not occur in my previous v0.9.6 workflow, where an `outlet()`-added transparency footer remained visible after reload. I can provide: - the complete `Outlet Persistence Probe v0.1.0`; - complete terminal logs for all four tests; - screenshots before and after reload; - the read-only SQLite inspection script and outputs.

title: Mobile sidebar chat list can be replaced by an empty later page
author: Shujakuinkuraudo
body:
### Check Existing Issues

- [x] I searched open and closed issues for related mobile sidebar and pagination reports.

### Open WebUI Version

v0.10.2 (`ecd48e2f718220a6400ecf49eafd4867a38feb10`)

### Installation Method

Docker

### Browser

Chrome on Android

### Expected Behavior

Opening the mobile sidebar after entering a chat should continue to show the first page of chat history.

### Actual Behavior

The chat history appears briefly and then disappears, leaving the Chats section empty. Search still finds and opens the same chats, so the records are not deleted.

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Have enough chat history for the sidebar's bottom loader to become visible.
2. Open the sidebar on a mobile-width viewport.
3. Enter an existing chat.
4. Open the sidebar again.
5. The history list briefly renders, then becomes empty.

### Request Sequence / Root Cause

Observed backend requests during one reproduction:

```text
GET /api/v1/chats/?page=1 200
GET /api/v1/chats/?page=2 200
# enter an existing chat and reopen the sidebar
GET /api/v1/chats/?page=2 200
GET /api/v1/chats/?page=2 200
```

`initChatList()` resets `currentChatPage` to 1, but it awaits several requests in `Promise.all`. The visible pagination loader can run concurrently and advance the shared store to page 2 before the chat-list callback evaluates `$currentChatPage`. The empty second page is then assigned to the global `chats` store, replacing the valid first page.

### Tested Fix

Use page `1` explicitly for the initialization request while leaving `loadMoreChats()` responsible for subsequent pages. This was built from v0.10.2 and deployed as a frontend override; the Android reproduction no longer occurs.

### Additional Information

No database changes are involved. Search and direct chat retrieval continue to work while the sidebar is empty.

title: feat: Show knowledge base folder structure when attaching knowledge in a chat
author: lecegues
body: ### Check Existing Issues - [x] I have searched all existing **open AND closed** issues and discussions and found none comparable to my request. ### Verify Feature Scope - [x] I believe this feature request is appropriately scoped for the Issues section as described above. ### Problem Description Knowledge bases support sub-folders/directories for organizing files, but the "Attach Knowledge" menu in the chat input lists every file in a **flat list**, completely ignoring file structure. This makes it hard to find a specific file when attaching from a large organized knowledge base. We have to screen every filename in the whole collection instead of navigating to the folder you know the file lives in. ### Proposed Solution When a knowledge base is expanded via "Show Files" in the chat menu, show its folder structure instead of a flat file list: 1. Go to "Attach Knowledge" in a chat 2. Press "Show Files" beside a knowledge base 3. See folder rows (with own "Show Files"-style expand control) alongside any root-level files 4. Pressing a folder reveals the files (and sub-folders) inside it, recursively Should mirror the same expand/collapse interaction the menu already uses for Knowledge Bases themselves. ### Alternatives Considered - Flat List with folder names as non-interactive section headers above their files. Simpler, but still won't scale well with bigger Knowledge Bases ### Additional Context _No response_

title: bug: Markdown paragraph swallows a following `<details>` block into a heading when the block content contains a bare `---`/`===` line
author: eliavb43
body:
### Summary When an assistant message contains prose text immediately followed by a `
` block (no blank line between them), and that `
` block's content contains a line that is *only* `---` or `===`, the renderer misinterprets the whole thing: instead of rendering a collapsed `
`/`` widget, it wraps the preceding text **and** the literal, HTML-escaped `
` tag source into one giant `

`/`

` heading. Everything after the swallowed heading renders as loose, unwrapped text instead of inside the collapsible widget. This is a real-world, day-to-day trigger — not an edge case — because `---`/`===` lines are extremely common in real content: `echo "---"` shell output, Markdown horizontal rules or table/heading underlines embedded in returned text, git conflict markers (`<<<<<<<`/`=======`), YAML frontmatter delimiters, etc. Any integration that streams command/tool output into a `
` block (e.g. a code-execution or tool-call plugin/pipe) will hit this regularly. ### Steps to reproduce 1. Get (or inject via the chat API) an assistant message with exactly this content: ``` Here is some introductory text before the widget.
Click to expand line one --- line two
``` 2. View the message in the chat UI. ### Expected behavior - "Here is some introductory text before the widget." renders as a normal paragraph. - A collapsed "Click to expand" `
` widget follows, which expands to show "line one / --- / line two". ### Actual behavior - The intro text AND the literal source of the `
` tag (e.g. `
`, `Click to expand`) get wrapped into one big `

` heading, rendered as literal escaped text (visibly showing the raw tag markup, not a collapsed widget). - "line two" and the closing `

` render below as plain, unwrapped text. ### Root cause / mechanism (as far as we could tell from the outside) This only reproduces when **both** conditions hold: 1. No blank line separates the preceding paragraph text from the `
` opening tag. 2. The `
` block's content contains a line consisting only of `---` or `===`. We verified both conditions are independently necessary with 3 minimal test cases: - No blank line + bare `---` inside → **broken** (swallowed into `

`). - Blank line before `
` + bare `---` inside → renders correctly (proper collapsed widget). - No blank line + no bare `---`/`===` anywhere in the block → renders correctly (proper collapsed widget). This looks like a paragraph/HTML-block interaction bug in the markdown pipeline: `
` should be able to interrupt a paragraph and start a raw-HTML block without a preceding blank line (this normally works fine — most `
` blocks without a blank line before them render correctly). But when a bare `---`/`===` line appears before the parser has "committed" to raw-HTML-block mode for that tag, it looks like the Setext-heading rule fires first and reinterprets the accumulated text (prose + literal tag source) as a heading, instead of the HTML-block rule taking precedence. ### Environment - Reproduced in the standard chat UI via a directly-injected chat history message (not dependent on any specific model, tool, or third-party plugin) — this is a frontend markdown rendering issue. - Not investigated: exact `marked.js`/DOMPurify version, or whether this depends on any deployment-specific customization of the renderer vs. stock config. ### Why this matters This makes the `
`-based "tool call" / "reasoning" collapsible-widget UI pattern fragile for any integration whose tool/command output can contain a bare `---`/`===` line — which is common — silently corrupting message rendering (leaking raw HTML source, breaking the collapse UI) with no error or warning.

title: issue: Analytics doesn't track the auto-generation features and RAG query generation
author: Vsono
body: ### Check Existing Issues - [x] I have searched for any existing and/or related issues. - [x] I have searched for any existing and/or related discussions. - [x] I have also searched in the CLOSED issues AND CLOSED discussions and found no related items (your issue might already be addressed on the development branch!). - [x] I am using the latest version of Open WebUI. ### Installation Method Pip Install ### Open WebUI Version 0.10.2 ### Ollama Version (if applicable) _No response_ ### Operating System Windows 11 (WSL) ### Browser (if applicable) _No response_ ### Confirmation - [x] I have read and followed all instructions in `README.md`. - [x] I am using the latest version of **both** Open WebUI and Ollama. - [x] I have included the browser console logs. - [x] I have included the Docker container logs. - [x] I have **provided every relevant configuration, setting, and environment variable used in my setup.** - [x] I have clearly **listed every relevant configuration, custom setting, environment variable, and command-line option that influences my setup** (such as Docker Compose overrides, .env values, browser settings, authentication configurations, etc). - [x] I have documented **step-by-step reproduction instructions that are precise, sequential, and leave nothing to interpretation**. My steps: - Start with the initial platform/version/OS and dependencies used, - Specify exact install/launch/configure commands, - List URLs visited, user input (incl. example values/emails/passwords if needed), - Describe all options and toggles enabled or changed, - Include any files or environmental changes, - Identify the expected and actual result at each stage, - Ensure any reasonably skilled user can follow and hit the same issue. ### Expected Behavior Analytics should track all of the API usage. ### Actual Behavior Analytics doesn't track token usage for Tags / Follow-Up / Title auto generation, or RAG query generation. ### Steps to Reproduce I'm using openrouter api. The endpoint I'm using is https://openrouter.ai/api/v1 1. (Optional) Add a new user, and disable the builtin tools so you can see the numbers better. 2. Make sure auto-generations are enabled in Settings. (Settings - Interface - Follow-Up Auto-Generation). 3. Send a message as the new user, if you did step 1, and wait until follow-up questions show up. 4. See that the token usage differs from what's shown on the provider's website. ### Logs & Screenshots On Open WebUI’s analytics dashboard: From the API request logs on the provider's website: Those are, respectively, for 1. Tags, 2. Follow-Up auto generation, 3. a direct answer to the user, and 4. Title auto generation. You can see the analytics only tracks the direct answer. I haven't uploaded an image, but it also doesn't track the query generation requests ("### Task: Analyze the chat history to determine the necessity of generating ..."). ### Additional Information _No response_

title: issue: Assistant messages are stored with empty content, causing conversation context loss in v0.10.2
author: EsliLeaL
body:
### Check Existing Issues

- [x] I have searched for any existing and/or related issues.
- [x] I have searched for any existing and/or related discussions.
- [x] I have also searched in the CLOSED issues AND CLOSED discussions and found no related items (your issue might already be addressed on the development branch!).
- [x] I am using the latest version of Open WebUI.

### Installation Method

Docker

### Open WebUI Version

0.10.2

### Ollama Version (if applicable)

0.31.1

### Operating System

Ubuntu 24.04 LTS (Docker)

### Browser (if applicable)

Edge

### Confirmation

- [x] I have read and followed all instructions in `README.md`.
- [x] I am using the latest version of **both** Open WebUI and Ollama.
- [x] I have included the browser console logs.
- [x] I have included the Docker container logs.
- [x] I have **provided every relevant configuration, setting, and environment variable used in my setup.**
- [x] I have clearly **listed every relevant configuration, custom setting, environment variable, and command-line option that influences my setup** (such as Docker Compose overrides, .env values, browser settings, authentication configurations, etc).
- [x] I have documented **step-by-step reproduction instructions that are precise, sequential, and leave nothing to interpretation**. My steps:
- Start with the initial platform/version/OS and dependencies used,
- Specify exact install/launch/configure commands,
- List URLs visited, user input (incl. example values/emails/passwords if needed),
- Describe all options and toggles enabled or changed,
- Include any files or environmental changes,
- Identify the expected and actual result at each stage,
- Ensure any reasonably skilled user can follow and hit the same issue.


### Expected Behavior

After an assistant response is generated, the complete conversation history should be preserved.

Example:

User:
Guarde este número: 7392.

Assistant:
Acknowledges the number.

User:
Qual foi o número que eu acabei de informar?

Expected response:

7392

The assistant should receive the full conversation history on every subsequent request.

### Actual Behavior

Conversation history is lost after every assistant response.

The issue reproduces consistently with multiple models, including:

- gemma4:e4b
- qwen3:8b

Running the same models directly through the Ollama CLI works correctly, indicating that the issue is not in Ollama itself.

We investigated the SQLite database and found that assistant messages are stored with:

```json
{
  "role": "assistant",
  "content": ""
}
```

while the actual response is stored only inside:

```text
output[].content[].text
```

As a result, the reconstructed conversation history contains assistant messages with empty content, causing the next request to lose the previous conversation context.

The issue reproduces consistently across multiple conversations.

No backend errors are present.

All requests return HTTP 200.

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Install Open WebUI v0.10.2 using Docker.

2. Connect Open WebUI to a local Ollama instance.

3. Start a new chat.

4. Select any model (tested with:
   - gemma4:e4b
   - qwen3:8b)

5. Send:

```
Guarde este número: 7392.
```

6. Wait for the assistant to answer.

7. Send:

```
Qual foi o número que eu acabei de informar?
```

8. Observe that the assistant behaves as if the previous conversation does not exist.

9. Execute the exact same test directly using the Ollama CLI:

```bash
ollama run gemma4:e4b
```

The model correctly remembers the previous message.

10. Inspect the Open WebUI SQLite database:

Assistant messages are stored with:

```json
{
  "role": "assistant",
  "content": ""
}
```

while the actual response text exists only inside:

```
output[].content[].text
```

This results in the reconstructed conversation history containing assistant messages with empty content.

### Logs & Screenshots

## Backend Logs

No backend errors were observed.

All requests completed successfully with HTTP 200.

Example:

```text
2026-07-11 12:41:33 POST /api/chat/completions HTTP/1.1 200
2026-07-11 12:41:58 POST /api/chat/completions HTTP/1.1 200
2026-07-11 12:42:29 POST /api/chat/completions HTTP/1.1 200
2026-07-11 12:42:55 POST /api/chat/completions HTTP/1.1 200
```

No exceptions or tracebacks are generated while reproducing the issue.

The problem appears to be related to conversation persistence rather than request processing.

### Additional Information

_No response_

title: issue: rapidocr missisng startup fails

body:
similar but different to: https://github.com/open-webui/open-webui/issues/26646
